summary: Explain the existing risk chips on Site Dashboard top-risk rows and Review Queue rows so staff can tell why those records are highlighted without changing routes or permissions.
selectedReason: Current repo evidence still matched the open handoff exactly: `workspace.js` showed risk chips like `High risk`, `Stale activity`, `No mentor`, and `Revision requested`, but those rows rarely explained what triggered them. The existing dashboard and Review Queue payloads already carried the reasons or flags, so the safest product-readiness batch was to add row-level explanation copy instead of inventing new actions or broadening filters.

candidateTable.decision: deferred: no site-backed audit destination is proven yet
deferred: backend queue rows are still absent
deferred: mutation and audit design still needed
deferred: hosted fake-account runtime is outside this local batch
deferred: broader workflow than this copy slice
not selected: valuable, but larger than this row-guidance batch

changes: Added a shared `renderRiskExplanation()` helper in `workspace.js` so protected row surfaces can explain active risk labels instead of only listing chips.
Updated Site Dashboard top-risk rows to explain why each student is highlighted using the existing `riskReasons` payload.
Updated Review Queue rows to explain why submitted, stale, revision, no-mentor, or missing-evidence records are highlighted using the existing `riskFlags` payload.
Added a human-readable `missing_evidence` risk label so current queue flags render cleanly.
Expanded `tests/workspace-app.test.mjs` to prove the new explanation text on both protected surfaces.
nextRecommended: Add a safe site-dashboard recent-activity destination only if a backed read-only path is proven, or revisit privacy-safe student-detail URL state.
doNotRepeat: Do not remove row-level risk explanation copy from Site Dashboard top-risk rows or Review Queue rows unless a clearer supported explanation replaces it.
Do not add a recent-activity click path until a site-backed destination and role visibility contract are proven.
